raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX I ordered XXXX items from a seller on XXXX. Unfortunately the items were not delivered. Subsequently on XX/XX/XXXX I contacted the seller but unfortunately I have received no response. On XX/XX/XXXX I contacted the Barclays card on purpose to dispute XXXX orders ( XXXX ) for XXXX and ( XXXX ) for {$3.00} for total of {$13.00}. Unfortunately the XXXX based XXXX demanded to know what was purchased regardless of the fact that info is private. Subsequently I requested to speak with the supervisor which after a very long hold I was told was not available. 

XXXX question to Barclays is this : is it collecting data on consumer spending habits that has nothing to do with the nature of the dispute process? If yes please disclose it in its terms and conditions and the way this info is monetized and if not what is the purpose of asking the questions such as this? 
The only pertaining info which should be asked would be if the item was ordered, ( yes ), the relevant order # ( yes ) and if the items arrived ( no ). 

It is very troubling to see Barclays outsourcing the jobs to the XXXX center overseas that have absolutely no consumer protections or basic service standards. As a result I closed the other card associated with Barclays ( AA Aviator ) as I will not accept a substandard and arrogant customer service. And since I am a frequent flyer with the AA both domestic and international I will express my dissatisfaction with the level of service provided by the credit card company. I also have to say with the AA card originally issued by the XXXX there were never any issues such as this.
